introduction: This spec defines the Admin Bootstrap component for apikit: the first-boot detection, admin token generation, admin email designation, file-presence guard, environment-variable validation, token rotation lifecycle, and auto-promotion hook called by the OAuth callback. Note: requirements referencing external library APIs (logrus, crypto/rand, crypto/subtle) are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against the installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.--admin-email: The CLI flag whose value designates the admin email address on first boot.
glossary.--reset-admin-token: The CLI flag that triggers token rotation on the current boot.
glossary.ADMIN_TOKEN: The environment variable that the operator must set on subsequent boots, containing the plaintext admin token for hash validation.
glossary.BootstrapParams: The Go struct passed to `Run()` that bundles all inputs for the bootstrap sequence.
glossary.INSERT OR REPLACE: An SQLite upsert statement used to write `admin_config` keys idempotently.
glossary.LoadConfig: The function from `01_server_core` / `internal/config` that loads `config.toml` and determines its directory.
glossary.Run: The primary exported function of the `internal/bootstrap` package that executes the full admin bootstrap sequence.
glossary.ShouldAutoPromote: The exported function in `internal/bootstrap` called by the OAuth callback handler to decide whether a newly created user should receive the `admin` role.
glossary.TokenPrefix: The build-time configurable string from `apikit.TokenPrefix` prepended to the admin token (default: `ak`).
glossary.XDG_CONFIG_HOME: An environment variable following the XDG Base Directory Specification; when set, determines the config and token file directory as `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit/`.
glossary.admin token: A break-glass infrastructure credential in the format `<prefix>_admin_<64 hex chars>` for emergency access â€” not for routine use.
glossary.admin_config: A key-value table in SQLite that stores singleton server state including the admin token hash and designated admin email.
glossary.admin_email: The key in `admin_config` that stores the designated administrator's email address.
glossary.admin_token: The plaintext token file written to the same directory as `config.toml`, containing the raw admin token string.
glossary.admin_token_hash: The key in `admin_config` that stores the SHA-256 hex digest of the admin token.
glossary.auto-promotion: The automatic granting of the `admin` role to a user whose email matches the designated `admin_email` on their first OAuth login.
glossary.break-glass: An emergency-only access mechanism bypassing normal authentication flows; the admin token is a break-glass credential.
glossary.config.toml: The primary apikit configuration file whose directory co-locates with the `admin_token` file.
glossary.crypto/rand: The Go standard library package providing cryptographically secure random byte generation.
glossary.crypto/sha256: The Go standard library package providing SHA-256 hash computation.
glossary.db.OpenMemory: A helper from `02_database_layer` that opens an in-memory SQLite database for testing.
glossary.file-presence guard: A startup check that refuses to start the server if the plaintext `admin_token` file still exists on disk, forcing the operator to secure and remove the file.
glossary.first boot: A server start where zero users exist in the `users` table, indicating the system has never been bootstrapped.
glossary.single-instance startup: The operational requirement that only one server instance runs the bootstrap sequence at a time, preventing concurrent writes to `admin_config`.
glossary.subtle.ConstantTimeCompare: The Go standard library function from `crypto/subtle` used for timing-safe byte-slice comparison.
glossary.token rotation: The process of generating a new admin token and invalidating the old one, triggered by the `--reset-admin-token` flag.
glossary.users: The SQLite table that stores registered user records; its row count determines whether this is a first boot.
glossary.AdminEmail: A field in `BootstrapParams` containing the value of the `--admin-email` flag; required on `first boot`, ignored on subsequent boots.
glossary.ResetToken: A boolean field in `BootstrapParams`; when true, triggers `token rotation` regardless of boot type.
glossary.ConfigDir: A field in `BootstrapParams` specifying the directory containing `config.toml`, used to resolve the `admin_token` file path.
glossary.admin: A user role value granting full access to all endpoints and resources; assigned via `auto-promotion` on `first boot` or manually by existing admins.
glossary.warn: A logrus log level used for important operational messages such as the `admin_token` file path on `first boot` and `token rotation`.
glossary.info: A logrus log level used for notable events such as `auto-promotion` of the designated admin user.
glossary.math/rand: The Go standard library pseudo-random number generator; explicitly prohibited for token generation in favor of `crypto/rand`.
glossary.02_database_layer: The database layer spec that provides the SQLite schema (including `users` and `admin_config` tables) and the `db.OpenMemory` test helper.
glossary.SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users: The SQL query used by `Run` to determine whether this is a `first boot` (zero rows) or a subsequent boot (one or more rows).
glossary.<prefix>_admin_<64 hex chars>: The format of the `admin token` string: the configurable `TokenPrefix`, followed by `_admin_`, followed by 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.
glossary.<TokenPrefix>_admin_<64 hex chars>: Equivalent to `<prefix>_admin_<64 hex chars>`; the `admin token` format using the `TokenPrefix` build-time variable.
glossary._admin_: The fixed infix in the `admin token` format that identifies the credential as an admin token, appearing between `TokenPrefix` and the 64 hex characters.
glossary.$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit/admin_token: The resolved path of the `admin_token` file when `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set in the environment.
glossary../admin_token: The resolved path of the `admin_token` file when `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is not set, relative to the current working directory.

user_story.goal: have the server automatically detect whether this is a first-boot situation
user_story.benefit: the correct bootstrap path runs without manual configuration beyond the first-boot flags

acceptance_criteria.action: queries `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users` after the database is opened and schema is created, before the HTTP server starts, to determine whether this is a `first boot`
classifies the current startup as a `first boot` and executes the first boot sequence
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the row count from `users` is zero

acceptance_criteria.action: classifies the current startup as a subsequent boot and executes the subsequent boot sequence
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the row count from `users` is greater than zero

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error; the server must not start
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error wrapping the database error
edge_cases.error_condition: the query to `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users` fails due to a database error

user_story.goal: have the server generate and store a secure admin token on first boot
user_story.benefit: I have an emergency break-glass credential from the moment the system is first started

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a non-nil error without starting the server
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-nil error with message `"first boot: --admin-email is required"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a `first boot` is detected and `AdminEmail` in `BootstrapParams` is empty

acceptance_criteria.action: stores the email in `admin_config` using `INSERT OR REPLACE` with key `admin_email`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a `first boot` is detected and a non-empty `AdminEmail` is provided

acceptance_criteria.action: generates 32 cryptographically random bytes via `crypto/rand`, hex-encodes them as 64 lowercase hex characters, and prepends `TokenPrefix` and `_admin_` to form the `admin token` string `<prefix>_admin_<64 hex chars>`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a `first boot` is detected

acceptance_criteria.action: computes the SHA-256 hash of the full token string using `crypto/sha256`, hex-encodes the 32-byte digest to a 64-character lowercase hex string, and stores it in `admin_config` with key `admin_token_hash` using `INSERT OR REPLACE`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `admin token` is generated on `first boot`

acceptance_criteria.action: writes the plaintext token to the `admin_token` file in the directory determined by `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (if set: `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit/admin_token`, otherwise `./admin_token`) with file mode 0600, overwriting any existing file
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `admin token` is generated on `first boot`

acceptance_criteria.action: logs the absolute path of the `admin_token` file at `warn` level with a message instructing the operator to save the token securely
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `admin_token` file is written on `first boot`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns nil, allowing the server to proceed to start the HTTP server
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the full first boot sequence completes without error

acceptance_criteria.action: contains the raw plaintext admin token string with no trailing newline

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error wrapping the `crypto/rand` error; no token is stored or written
edge_cases.error_condition: `crypto/rand` fails to generate random bytes during `first boot`

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error wrapping the OS error; the server must not start
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error wrapping the os.WriteFile error
edge_cases.error_condition: writing the `admin_token` file fails (e.g., permission denied, disk full) during `first boot`

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error; the server must not start
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error wrapping the database error
edge_cases.error_condition: storing `admin_email` or `admin_token_hash` in `admin_config` fails due to a database error during `first boot`

edge_cases.action: never calls os.Exit or log.Fatal internally; all errors are returned to the caller as non-nil error values so that the server binary's main() function controls process termination

user_story.goal: automatically receive the admin role when I first log in via OAuth
user_story.benefit: I do not need a separate manual role-grant step to manage the system

acceptance_criteria.action: returns true, indicating the newly created user should be granted the `admin` role
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with an email that matches the value stored in `admin_config` under key `admin_email`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns false
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with an email that does not match the stored `admin_email`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns false with a nil error
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called when no `admin_email` key exists in `admin_config`

acceptance_criteria.action: is only meaningful for newly created users (first OAuth login); callers (the OAuth callback handler) are responsible for not calling it on updates to existing users
logs the auto-promotion event at `info` level, identifying the promoted user's email
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `ShouldAutoPromote` returns true and the OAuth callback grants the `admin` role to the new user

edge_cases.action: returns (false, error) wrapping the database error
edge_cases.error_condition: the database query to read `admin_email` from `admin_config` fails

user_story.goal: have the server verify I have secured the admin token before starting on any non-first boot
user_story.benefit: the plaintext admin token cannot persist on disk accidentally across restarts

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a non-nil error instructing the operator to save the token securely and delete the file; the server must not start
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-nil error with message matching `"admin_token file exists at %s: save the token securely and delete the file before restarting"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a subsequent boot is detected and the `admin_token` file exists on disk

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a non-nil error indicating that `ADMIN_TOKEN` is required
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-nil error with message `"ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is required"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a subsequent boot is detected, the `admin_token` file is absent, and `ADMIN_TOKEN` environment variable is empty or unset

acceptance_criteria.action: computes the SHA-256 hash of the `ADMIN_TOKEN` value, hex-encodes it, and compares it against `admin_token_hash` from `admin_config` using `subtle.ConstantTimeCompare`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a subsequent boot is detected, the `admin_token` file is absent, and `ADMIN_TOKEN` is set

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a non-nil error indicating the admin token is invalid
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-nil error with message `"ADMIN_TOKEN does not match the stored admin token"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the SHA-256 hash of `ADMIN_TOKEN` does not match the stored `admin_token_hash`

acceptance_criteria.action: silently ignores the `--admin-email` value without logging a warning or modifying the stored `admin_email`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a subsequent boot is detected and `AdminEmail` is non-empty in `BootstrapParams`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns nil, allowing the server to proceed to start the HTTP server
acceptance_criteria.trigger: hash comparison succeeds on a subsequent boot

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a non-nil error instructing the operator to run with `--reset-admin-token`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-nil error with message `"no admin token hash found in database; run with --reset-admin-token to generate a new token"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a subsequent boot is detected and `admin_token_hash` key is absent from `admin_config`

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error wrapping the database error
edge_cases.error_condition: reading `admin_token_hash` from `admin_config` fails due to a database error on a subsequent boot

edge_cases.action: uses `subtle.ConstantTimeCompare` for all token hash comparisons to prevent timing side-channel attacks

user_story.goal: rotate the admin token using the --reset-admin-token flag
user_story.benefit: I can generate a fresh break-glass credential even when the current token may be compromised, without needing a running server

acceptance_criteria.action: runs `token rotation` regardless of user count: generates a new `admin token` using the same flow as `first boot` (32 random bytes via `crypto/rand`, hex-encode, prepend `TokenPrefix`)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `ResetToken` is true in `BootstrapParams`

acceptance_criteria.action: overwrites `admin_token_hash` in `admin_config` with the SHA-256 hash of the new token using `INSERT OR REPLACE`, immediately invalidating the old token
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a new `admin token` is generated during `token rotation`

acceptance_criteria.action: writes the new plaintext token to the `admin_token` file (same path resolution, mode 0600), overwriting any existing file
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a new `admin token` is generated during `token rotation`

acceptance_criteria.action: logs the absolute path of the `admin_token` file at `warn` level with a message instructing the operator to save the token securely
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `admin_token` file is written during `token rotation`

acceptance_criteria.action: skips the `file-presence guard` and `ADMIN_TOKEN` environment variable check for this boot and returns nil
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `token rotation` completes without error

acceptance_criteria.action: applies the `file-presence guard` and `ADMIN_TOKEN` environment variable validation as a normal subsequent boot
acceptance_criteria.state: the next restart after a `token rotation` boot, without `--reset-admin-token`

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error wrapping the `crypto/rand` error; the old `admin_token_hash` in `admin_config` is not modified
returns a non-nil error wrapping the OS error; the server must not start
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error wrapping the os.WriteFile error
edge_cases.error_condition: writing the `admin_token` file fails during `token rotation`

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error; the server must not start
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error wrapping the database error

user_story.goal: confirm the admin token is generated and stored using cryptographically sound methods
user_story.benefit: the token cannot be guessed or derived, and timing attacks on comparison are prevented

acceptance_criteria.action: produces tokens in the format `<TokenPrefix>_admin_<64 hex chars>` where the 64 hex chars are the lowercase hex encoding of 32 bytes from `crypto/rand`
applies `crypto/sha256` to the full token string including the prefix, then hex-encodes the 32-byte digest to produce a 64-character lowercase hex string stored in `admin_token_hash`
uses `subtle.ConstantTimeCompare` to compare the hex-encoded SHA-256 hash of the candidate token against the stored `admin_token_hash`
never uses `math/rand`; all randomness comes from `crypto/rand`
contains only the raw plaintext admin token string with no trailing newline, making it safe for use with shell command substitution

requirements.title: Bootstrap Execution Order and Single-Instance Constraint

user_story.goal: have the bootstrap sequence run at the correct point during server startup
user_story.benefit: the database is ready before bootstrap logic executes, and the HTTP server only starts after bootstrap succeeds

acceptance_criteria.action: executes bootstrap via `Run` after `LoadConfig` completes and after the database is opened and schema is created by `02_database_layer`, but before the HTTP server begins accepting requests
executes `token rotation` first, then returns without running the first-boot or subsequent-boot sequence
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `ResetToken` is true

acceptance_criteria.action: treats the error as fatal and does not start the HTTP server; the process exits with a non-zero status
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `Run` returns a non-nil error

acceptance_criteria.action: must ensure only a single server instance executes the bootstrap sequence at a time, particularly on `first boot` and during `token rotation`; concurrent `Run` calls against the same database are not supported

edge_cases.action: never calls os.Exit, log.Fatal, or any function that terminates the process; all failure conditions are signaled by returning a non-nil error to the caller

user_story.goal: have the admin_token file placed predictably alongside config.toml
user_story.benefit: I can reliably find and delete the file after securing the token

acceptance_criteria.action: resolves the `admin_token` file path to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit/admin_token`
resolves the `admin_token` file path to `./admin_token` (current working directory), consistent with how `LoadConfig` resolves `config.toml`
uses the `ConfigDir` field of `BootstrapParams` to construct the absolute path to the `admin_token` file

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error wrapping the OS error
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error wrapping the OS error
edge_cases.error_condition: the directory for the `admin_token` file does not exist and cannot be created

correctness_properties.title: Token Uniqueness
correctness_properties.invariant: the two token strings are different with overwhelming probability (each draws 32 bytes from `crypto/rand`)

correctness_properties.title: Token Hash Determinism
correctness_properties.invariant: hashing it twice with `crypto/sha256` and hex-encoding produces the same 64-character lowercase hex string

correctness_properties.title: Token Hash Discrimination
correctness_properties.invariant: their SHA-256 hex digests are different, so the wrong token never passes the `admin_token_hash` comparison

correctness_properties.title: File-Presence Guard Blocks Start
correctness_properties.for_any: subsequent boot attempt where the `admin_token` file is present on disk
correctness_properties.invariant: `Run` returns a non-nil error and the HTTP server never starts

correctness_properties.title: Old Token Invalidated After Rotation
correctness_properties.invariant: the SHA-256 hash of the previous `admin token` no longer matches `admin_token_hash` in `admin_config`

correctness_properties.title: First Boot Requires Admin Email
correctness_properties.for_any: first boot attempt with an empty `AdminEmail` in `BootstrapParams`
correctness_properties.invariant: `Run` returns a non-nil error and neither `admin_email` nor `admin_token_hash` is written to `admin_config`

correctness_properties.title: ShouldAutoPromote Is Idempotent on No-Config
correctness_properties.for_any: call to `ShouldAutoPromote` when no `admin_email` key exists in `admin_config`
correctness_properties.invariant: the function returns (false, nil) without error regardless of the email argument

correctness_properties.title: Run Never Terminates the Process
correctness_properties.invariant: `Run` signals failure by returning a non-nil error and never calls os.Exit or log.Fatal

execution_paths.title: First Boot: Operator Starts Fresh Server

steps.action: runs the server binary with `--admin-email admin@example.com` against a database with zero users
calls `LoadConfig` (from `01_server_core`) to load `config.toml` and determine `ConfigDir`
opens the SQLite database and creates the schema including the `users` and `admin_config` tables
queries `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users`; receives 0; classifies as `first boot`
stores `admin@example.com` in `admin_config` under key `admin_email` via `INSERT OR REPLACE`
generates 32 random bytes via `crypto/rand`, forms the `admin token` `<TokenPrefix>_admin_<64 hex chars>`
computes SHA-256 hash of the token, hex-encodes it, stores it in `admin_config` under `admin_token_hash`
writes the plaintext token to the `admin_token` file at the resolved path with mode 0600
logs the absolute path of the `admin_token` file at `warn` level; returns nil
starts the HTTP server and begins accepting requests

execution_paths.title: Subsequent Boot: Operator Provides Valid ADMIN_TOKEN

steps.action: deletes the `admin_token` file, sets `ADMIN_TOKEN` env var to the saved token, and restarts the server
calls `LoadConfig` and opens the database via `02_database_layer`
queries `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users`; receives a positive count; classifies as subsequent boot
checks for `admin_token` file presence; file is absent; continues
reads `ADMIN_TOKEN` from the environment; computes SHA-256 hash; compares with `admin_token_hash` in `admin_config` via `subtle.ConstantTimeCompare`
hashes match; returns nil
starts the HTTP server and begins accepting requests

execution_paths.title: Subsequent Boot: File-Presence Guard Blocks Startup

steps.action: restarts the server without deleting the `admin_token` file
queries `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users`; classifies as subsequent boot
detects the `admin_token` file still exists on disk
returns a non-nil error with message instructing the operator to save the token and delete the file
calls log.Fatal with the error; process exits with non-zero status; HTTP server never starts

execution_paths.title: Token Rotation: Operator Resets Admin Token

steps.action: restarts the server with `--reset-admin-token` flag
calls `LoadConfig` and opens the database via `02_database_layer`; sets `ResetToken: true` in `BootstrapParams`
detects `ResetToken` is true; runs `token rotation` immediately without checking user count
generates a new `admin token`; computes and stores new `admin_token_hash` via `INSERT OR REPLACE`, invalidating the old token
writes the new plaintext token to the `admin_token` file (mode 0600); logs the path at `warn` level
skips `file-presence guard` and `ADMIN_TOKEN` check for this boot; returns nil
starts the HTTP server normally for this boot
saves the new token, deletes the `admin_token` file, sets `ADMIN_TOKEN` to the new value before the next restart

execution_paths.title: Auto-Promotion: Designated Admin Email First OAuth Login

steps.action: authenticates via OAuth for the first time using the email address stored in `admin_email`
creates a new user record and calls `ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, sqlDB, email)`
queries `admin_config` for the `admin_email` key; finds a match with the provided email; returns (true, nil)
grants the `admin` role to the newly created user
logs the auto-promotion event at `info` level identifying the promoted user's email

execution_paths.title: Full End-to-End: First Boot Through Subsequent Boot With OAuth Auto-Promotion

steps.action: runs `server --admin-email admin@corp.com` for the first time against an empty database
calls `LoadConfig` (01_server_core) to load `config.toml` and populate `ConfigDir` and `TokenPrefix`
opens the SQLite database via `db.Open` and applies schema migrations creating `users` and `admin_config` tables
detects zero rows in `users`; runs first boot sequence: stores `admin_email`, generates token, stores `admin_token_hash`, writes `admin_token` file, logs path at `warn`; returns nil
starts the HTTP server; begins accepting OAuth callback requests
saves the admin token from the `admin_token` file, then deletes the file
user with email `admin@corp.com` completes OAuth login for the first time
creates the user record and calls `ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, sqlDB, "admin@corp.com")`
finds `admin_email = admin@corp.com` in `admin_config`; returns (true, nil)
sets `role: admin` on the new user record; server logs auto-promotion at `info`
restarts the server with `ADMIN_TOKEN` environment variable set to the saved token
detects users exist; runs subsequent boot: `file-presence guard` passes (file deleted), `ADMIN_TOKEN` hash matches `admin_token_hash`; returns nil
starts the HTTP server normally; system is fully operational

error_handling.condition: First boot detected and `--admin-email` flag not provided (empty `AdminEmail` in `BootstrapParams`)
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error with message `"first boot: --admin-email is required"`; the server does not start

error_handling.condition: `admin_token` file exists on disk during a subsequent boot (not a reset boot)
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error with message `"admin_token file exists at %s: save the token securely and delete the file before restarting"`; the server does not start

error_handling.condition: `ADMIN_TOKEN` environment variable is empty or unset on a subsequent boot
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error with message `"ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is required"`; the server does not start

error_handling.condition: SHA-256 hash of `ADMIN_TOKEN` does not match `admin_token_hash` stored in `admin_config`
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error with message `"ADMIN_TOKEN does not match the stored admin token"`; the server does not start

error_handling.condition: `admin_token_hash` key not found in `admin_config` on a subsequent boot
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error with message `"no admin token hash found in database; run with --reset-admin-token to generate a new token"`; the server does not start

error_handling.condition: `crypto/rand` fails to generate random bytes during token generation
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error wrapping the `crypto/rand` error; no token or hash is written; the server does not start

error_handling.condition: Writing the `admin_token` file fails (e.g., permission denied, disk full)
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error wrapping the os.WriteFile error; the server does not start

error_handling.condition: Database error when querying `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users`
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error; bootstrap sequence is aborted; the server does not start

error_handling.condition: Database error when reading or writing `admin_config` (any key)
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error; the server does not start
`ShouldAutoPromote` returns (false, error) wrapping the database error; the caller (OAuth callback) is responsible for handling the error

error_handling.condition: The directory for the `admin_token` file does not exist and cannot be created
error_handling.behavior: `Run` returns a non-nil error wrapping the OS error; the server does not start

symbols.name: Logger
symbols.notes: Structured logger instance passed via BootstrapParams.Logger; used for Warn and Info level log calls

symbols.name: Logger.Warnf
symbols.notes: Used to log admin_token file path on first boot and token rotation

symbols.name: Read
symbols.notes: Used to generate 32 cryptographically random bytes for the admin token

symbols.name: Sum256
symbols.notes: Used to compute the SHA-256 hash of the admin token string

symbols.name: ConstantTimeCompare
symbols.notes: Used for timing-safe comparison of token hash byte slices; returns 1 if equal, 0 if not

symbols.name: EncodeToString
symbols.notes: Used to hex-encode random bytes and SHA-256 digests

symbols.name: WriteFile
symbols.signature: func WriteFile(name string, data []byte, perm fs.FileMode) error
symbols.notes: Used to write the plaintext admin token to the admin_token file with mode 0600

symbols.name: Stat
symbols.notes: Used to check for existence of the admin_token file during the file-presence guard

symbols.name: Getenv
symbols.notes: Used to read the ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable
